hard.next_step: Confidence 75% 3rd Jul- End of July customer will be concluded all evaluations for the competition from Demo point of view our position it was good and some tech queries need response from our team , additionally we need to provide revised quotes via KPMG and Accenture and KPMG as per customer request . 23rd of June 2026 - scheduled for live Demo SRM , and we will discuss our position with the RFP and will discuss SIs and DC . 6th Jun Ongoing clarifications with advisor (Franck) , references, DC, info Sec etc 21st May- Franck reached out to get clarification on "on prem vs cloud" response in our RFP- ck has responded 13th May - RFP Clarifications (~600) submitted and receipt clarification received .... considering the complexity expecting this will be an iterative process. 4th May- RFP submitted- clarification questions now received (extensive)- asked for extension to respond- accepted request. Significant effort required to respond with utmost accuracy 25th Mar- RFP extension advised- closing April 21st (EY has agreed to prime and supply 30% local Saudi resources, as required) 13th Mar- RFP is now out and under review by team- 2 weeks to seek clarifications and address internal pros/cons 2nd Mar Regional military disruption in region- however SAMI have confirmed Zycus is shortlisted (confidentially, to 3) RFP to come out in next 1 week All further meetings to be approved by CK in ME 26th Feb- Positive presentation/workshop (4 hours). Process is better defined- currently evaluating internally (Zycus team) on definitive fit Team to huddle this week and work through next step strategies. 3 vendors in mix, all will be similar fit to Zycus- we need edge here- and currently investigating how we can drive a strong AI message and solution 10th Feb- good interaction with team- demo confirmed 23rd Feb (STE is pivotal) 30th Jan Updated from SAMI (Franck)- we are invited to do presentations (they call Innovation Contest) week - 22nf Feb-26th Feb Details/expectations of demos due next 2 days. 24th Jan - RFI submitted, next steps live demos early Feb. 22nd Jan - Clarifications received a week late, working on the RFI response due Saturday 13th Jan ck met with SAMI team F2F- very positive intro and overview focused on STE- current FRO out and responding by 20th Jan 22nd Dec - Confirmed participation, next step is clarifications (deadline 8th Jan) and RFI build - some big gaps, but could be doable with PNG support. 16th Dec - RFI received for full S2P - deadline is 24th Jan , Carl also reached out and arranged a ST Engineering overview on the 13th. 15th Dec - NDA received by Viraat (Alliances) in order to receive an RFI for 'SRM' - signed and sent with follow up highlighting ST Engineering Confirm Zycus participation in SAMI SRM RFI by 22 Dec 2025 , raise SFDC opportunity, initiate cross-functional RFI response team, and begin review of RFI requirements and compliance mapping ahead of Jan 2026 submission milestones.
hard.ais_why:
• The 26 Feb 2026 rep note explicitly captures the AI-positioning intent: 'Positive presentation/workshop (4 hours). Process is better defined- currently evaluating internally (Zycus team) on definitive fit. Team to huddle this week and work through next step strategies. 3 vendors in mix, all will be similar fit to Zycus- we need edge here- and currently investigating how we can drive a strong AI message and solution.' AI is Zycus's chosen differentiator, not buyer's stated criterion.
• On 4 May 2026: 'RFP submitted- clarification questions now received (extensive)- asked for extension to respond.' On 13 Mar 2026: 'RFP is now out and under review by team- 2 weeks to seek clarifications.' Per 13 Jan 2026: 'ck met with SAMI team F2F- very positive intro and overview focused on STE.' ST Engineering reference (a Zycus customer using the full Agentic suite) was 'pivotal' in shortlisting. AI products are fully in scope (ML iAnalyze, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA).
• Confidence is Medium. SAMI carries the strongest AI product loadout in chunk 1 (6 AI SKUs), and is shortlisted to top-3 in a Saudi defence/aerospace RFP with EY priming for local Saudi resources. But the AI narrative is explicitly rep-driven ('drive a strong AI message'), not buyer-originated, and BD_Engagement is Unengaged. Held DOWN at 6 boundary per rubric.

north_star_verdict.headline: $2.2M Saudi defense S2P shortlisted to 3 with a strong 23 Jun demo and an end-July decision, but the 29 Aug close is optimistic: partner selection (EY vs Accenture/KPMG), AEC on-prem hosting rework, revised quotes, InfoSec, and legal all sit after selection.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-12-18, 200 days old; in Shortlisted since ~13 Mar (RFP out), ~115 days in stage. Close 2026-08-29 is 54 days out. SAMI's stated decision window is end-of-July (Next_Step 03 Jul), ~14-25 days away. CloseDate already slipped once, 2026-06-29 to 2026-08-29 (field history 07 Jun). Post-selection this defense deal still needs: revised partner-based quotes via Accenture/KPMG, AEC on-prem hosting-compliant proposal, budget-owner sign-off on $2.2M, InfoSec/DC review, MSA/SOW/legal, and S/4HANA integration scoping. Defense enterprise selection-to-signature norm is 90-120 days; a late-July selection leaves ~30 days to 29 Aug, which is tight. Deal is healthy and engaged, so the sole real problem is the date.
evidence: Last two-way buyer touch: 23 Jun SRM live demo (Avoma) with Franck Devienne and evaluators; buyer confirmed end-of-July decision window and requested revised proposals.

Next_Step__c updated 2026-07-03 by Carl Kimball: 'Confidence 75%, end of July customer will conclude all evaluations, our position on the demo was good, some tech queries need response, and we need to provide revised quotes via KPMG and Accenture per customer request.'
CloseDate slipped 2026-06-29 to 2026-08-29 (OpportunityFieldHistory 2026-06-07, by Carl Kimball) and Amount raised 1,200,000 to 2,200,000 (2026-03-25) as scope expanded to full S2P.
Shortlisted to 3 finalists in a formal defense RFP (Next_Step 02 Mar 'shortlisted, confidentially, to 3') against GEP Worldwide, Coupa, SAP Ariba, iValua (SF Competitors__c).
New buyer requirement: SAMI wants Zycus hosted at AEC, its own defense subsidiary data center, treated as on-premise (Franck emails 16-22 Jun); Zycus committed to a revised AEC-compliant proposal.

New buyer requirement: SAMI will select the SRM solution AND the implementor, and wants alternative partner proposals from Accenture and KPMG (its SAP rollout integrators) beside EY (Franck 19-22 Jun); Zycus working three partner proposals.

No budget owner above Franck engaged: on the 23 Jun demo an executive attendee probed strategy and cross-functional approvals, signalling a higher authority must sign off on $2.2M, but no C-level SAMI contact appears in any role, call, or task.

metrics.narrative: SAMI has articulated clear operational and strategic pain but has not quantified ROI or savings. The pain is defense supply-chain complexity plus Saudi Vision 2030 localization: on the 23 Jun demo the buyer pressed hard on a local-content dashboard against the 50% Vision 2030 target, GAMI segment scoring, iCV supplier scores, and defense-grade supplier certification/traceability. These are real, specific business problems, but no SAMI stakeholder stated a cost of the current state, a cycle-time figure, an error rate, or an expected savings number on any call or field. The financial case is therefore unquantified. To move to confirmed, Zycus must co-build a value case with SAMI (localization-compliance value, sourcing-cycle savings, supplier-consolidation savings) and put it in front of the budget owner with the commercial proposal.
sources: Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23 (local-content dashboard, GAMI LC scoring, iCV, defense certification discussion)
Next_Step__c 2026-02-26 '4-hour workshop, process better defined, evaluating internally on fit'
SF AIS_Why__c

economic_buyer.narrative: Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor) orchestrates the commercial track: he drives the RFP clarifications, chases the revised proposals ('when shall we expect ZYCUS updated proposal based on AEC hosting?', email 22 Jun), and owns partner/pricing direction. But Franck is an advisor, not the budget owner. On the 23 Jun demo a senior SAMI attendee (unnamed in Salesforce) probed cross-functional approval chains and strategy, signalling final sign-off on the $2.2M sits above procurement, likely with a SAMI executive or GAMI-aligned authority. No such person appears in any OpportunityContactRole, task, or named call speaker, so the economic buyer is not verifiable in Salesforce. Identifying and reaching that budget owner before the end-July selection is the single biggest power-mapping move.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Franck Devienne, Yousef Alarabi, Ahmed Almania; no EB role)
Franck Devienne emails 2026-06-16 to 2026-06-22 (owns proposal/pricing cadence)
Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23 (executive probing approvals)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well evidenced from the RFP and the 23 Jun demo. SAMI is scoring on: strategic sourcing depth (two-envelope RFQ/RFP, reverse/English/Japanese auctions), defense-grade supplier management (line-level certifications, classified-material approval, regulatory validation, supplier prequalification), Saudi localization (a GAMI-compliant local-content dashboard against the Vision 2030 50% target, iCV scoring, LCGPA export format), SAP S/4HANA integration (master data, MRP, push-back of awarded prices), no-code configurability of forms and multi-function approval workflows, and hosting/data-residency (AEC on-prem). Franck explicitly steered the demo to the product deep-dive over commercials, confirming fit-to-requirement is the current gate. These are confirmed from the buyer's own questions on the call.
sources: Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23 (Junaid on defense certification, cross-functional approvals, local-content dashboard, SAP integration)
Next_Step__c (RFP ~600 clarifications, two-envelope structure)

decision_process.narrative: SAMI is running a structured, arms-length competitive RFP: NDA and RFI (Dec 2025), RFI submission (24 Jan), 'Innovation Contest' demos (Feb), formal RFP out (13 Mar) with ~600 clarifications (submitted 13 May), extended twice, and a final SRM live-demo round (23 Jun) run identically across all vendors. Franck controls cadence and vendor communication and gated all meetings through the ME team. A distinctive process fork emerged in June: SAMI will select the SRM solution AND the implementation partner together, concurrent with its SAP rollout (Accenture and KPMG are SAMI's SAP integrators), so partner choice is now inside the decision. SAMI states all evaluations conclude end of July; commercial/legal/InfoSec/hosting steps follow selection.
sources: Next_Step__c timeline (Dec 2025 to Jul 2026)
Franck Devienne email 2026-06-19 'SAMI will select the SRM solution AND the implementor... concurrent with SAP rollout, ACCENTURE and KPMG'
Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly visible and materially complex for a Saudi defense buyer. Two open threads dominate: (1) hosting/data residency, where SAMI wants Zycus hosted at AEC (a SAMI defense subsidiary, treated as on-premise) rather than cloud, forcing a revised technical/commercial proposal and a landing-zone review; and (2) implementation-partner contracting, where SAMI wants proposals from Accenture and KPMG beside EY, meaning the SOW/delivery paper is not yet settled. InfoSec, data centre (DC), and reference checks are noted as ongoing (Next_Step 06 Jun). No MSA/SOW draft, legal channel, or signatory is yet evidenced. Visibility will come from confirming SAMI's preferred partner and hosting model in writing and mapping the legal/procurement path after selection.
sources: Franck Devienne emails 2026-06-16 to 2026-06-22 (AEC hosting, partner proposals)
Next_Step__c 2026-06-06 'ongoing clarifications with advisor (Franck), references, DC, InfoSec etc'

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is concrete and defense-specific. On the 23 Jun demo SAMI's technical evaluator (Junaid) pressed on managing classified/regulated materials, supplier certifications and approval by regulatory bodies, and segregating defense-approved catalogs, describing today's manual, error-prone checking of material standards against regulatory documentation. Layered on top is a mandated Saudi localization obligation: continuous local-content measurement against GAMI segments and the Vision 2030 50% target, with LCGPA reporting. The pain owner is SAMI procurement (Franck/Yousef) plus the defense/compliance function represented by the AEC and defense-sector participants. This pain is confirmed from the buyer's own words on the call, not inferred.
sources: Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23 (Junaid: classified materials, regulatory approval, defense certification; local-content/GAMI/iCV discussion)

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Franck Devienne is engaged, professional, and gives Zycus fair airtime, but he runs a neutral, competitive RFP and a 15 May CRM note records 'Carl has had positive conversations with Franck but he doesn't fit champion role.' Yousef Alarabi and Ahmed Almania are transactional evaluators. No SAMI stakeholder has advocated internally for Zycus, shared competitor intelligence, or pushed our differentiators. Without a champion, Zycus has no line of sight into where GEP or the others stand, or into the budget owner's preferences, 2-3 weeks before selection. Building an internal advocate (most plausibly a business/defense sponsor who values the localization and AI story) is the highest-leverage relationship move now.
sources: CRM task 2026-05-15 'Franck... doesn't fit champion role'
OpportunityContactRole (no champion role)
Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23 (no advocacy behaviour)

competition.narrative: This is a live, formal 3-finalist defense RFP. The named field is GEP Worldwide, Coupa, SAP Ariba, and iValua (SF Competitors__c); SAMI confirmed a shortlist to 3 (Next_Step 02 Mar). GEP is the strongest current threat given proven defense/ERP-integration pedigree, which bears directly on SAMI's open S/4HANA and defense-supplier questions. Zycus's 23 Jun demo landed well ('our position was good', Next_Step 03 Jul), and the wedge is the full agentic-AI suite (Merlin negotiation/intake, ANA, ML iAnalyze) plus EY/partner local Saudi delivery and a tailored GAMI local-content dashboard. The risk is that AI is Zycus-driven, not buyer-stated (AIS_Why), and on the demo the buyer noted the core sourcing flow is not AI-dependent, so the AI edge must be made explicitly relevant to win.
sources: SF Competitors__c (GEP Worldwide, Coupa, iValua, SAP Ariba)
Next_Step__c 2026-03-02 'shortlisted, confidentially, to 3'
Next_Step__c 2026-07-03 'our position on the demo was good'
Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23

deal_movement.summary: Active and progressing: a 23 Jun SRM live demo landed well, SAMI is pulling revised proposals (AEC hosting, Accenture/KPMG partner quotes), and set an end-July decision. CloseDate slipped once (29 Jun to 29 Aug) and scope/amount expanded to $2.2M full S2P.

SAMI requested revised proposal for AEC on-prem hosting (defense subsidiary data centre)
SAMI expanded partner scope: wants Accenture and KPMG implementation proposals beside EY
CloseDate slipped from 2026-06-29 to 2026-08-29 (by Carl Kimball)
Amount raised 1,200,000 to 2,200,000 and forecast set to Best Case as scope grew to full S2P
SAMI confirmed Zycus shortlisted (confidentially) to 3 finalists

competitive_position.summary: Live 3-finalist formal defense RFP. GEP Worldwide is the strongest current threat, its defense/ERP-integration pedigree speaking directly to SAMI's open S/4HANA and defense-supplier questions; Coupa, SAP Ariba, and iValua complete the named field (SF Competitors__c) with no buyer-stated preference for any of them. Zycus's 23 Jun demo landed well and our wedge is the full agentic-AI suite plus EY/partner local Saudi delivery and a GAMI local-content dashboard, but AI is our narrative not the buyer's stated criterion, so the edge must be made explicit before the end-July decision.

competitors.quote: 3 vendors in mix, all will be similar fit to Zycus, we need edge here
competitors.how_we_win: Out-integrate on defense S/4HANA (consignment/MRP, classified-material lineage) and lead with Merlin agentic AI plus EY local delivery as the differentiated edge.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: SAMI confirmed Zycus is shortlisted (confidentially, to 3)
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast configurability and no-code workflow depth against Ariba complexity; SAMI's SAP rollout is via Accenture/KPMG, not an Ariba lock-in.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: SAMI confirmed Zycus is shortlisted (confidentially, to 3)
competitors.how_we_win: Lead on defense-grade supplier certification, localization dashboard, and AEC on-prem hosting flexibility that a pure-cloud vendor cannot match.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: SAMI confirmed Zycus is shortlisted (confidentially, to 3)
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on agentic-AI breadth and delivered Saudi/defense references (ST Engineering) versus a lighter regional footprint.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on strategic sourcing, supplier management, no-code workflows, and the Saudi local-content dashboard demonstrated 23 Jun; the exposed edges are AEC on-prem hosting, defense-grade S/4HANA integration, and third-party regulatory validation.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers before the end-July decision: reach the budget owner, sharpen the AI wedge, and lock one clean partner-and-hosting proposal so GEP cannot exploit integration/delivery uncertainty.
flags: Single-threaded through procurement advisor Franck; map and reach the SAMI budget owner and a business/defense sponsor before the end-July selection.

AI is our narrative, not the buyer's stated criterion, and the demo showed core flows are not AI-dependent; make Merlin agentic AI explicitly relevant to SAMI's localization and defense-supplier pain.
Partner and hosting story is in flux (EY vs Accenture/KPMG; AEC on-prem); consolidate into one clean revised proposal so GEP's defense-integration pedigree cannot become the tiebreaker.

items.name: Franck Devienne
items.title: Senior Procurement Advisor
items.risk: Runs a formal, competitive, arms-length RFP and owns proposal/pricing cadence; engaged but not an advocate, so Zycus has no inside read on competitor standing.

items.name: Yousef Alarabi
items.title: Sr. Procurement Specialist
items.risk: Named evaluator and recipient of the revised DC/quote correspondence; transactional, not yet an advocate.

items.name: Ahmed Almania
items.title: Procurement Specialist
items.risk: Procurement evaluator on the RFP/demo; limited direct engagement, no advocacy observed.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Franck Devienne is engaged and fair but explicitly not a champion (CRM 15 May); Yousef and Ahmed are transactional evaluators. With selection ~3 weeks out, the absence of an internal advocate leaves Zycus blind to competitor positioning and the budget owner's preferences.
champion_strength.source: CRM task 2026-05-15 + OpportunityContactRole (no champion) + Avoma SRM demo 2026-06-23

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Full agentic-AI suite is in scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA, ML iAnalyze) and Zycus has chosen AI as its differentiator, but on the 23 Jun demo the buyer noted the core sourcing flow is not AI-dependent, so the AI edge is under-leveraged against SAMI's actual localization and defense-supplier criteria.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 Moderate: rep-driven AI narrative ('drive a strong AI message'), not buyer-originated. The buyer engaged AI features neutrally on the demo and confirmed core flows work without AI, so appetite is curious, not hungry.

items.detail: Budget owner above Franck is unmapped and unengaged 2-3 weeks before selection on a $2.2M defense deal.
Open defense-grade S/4HANA and classified-supplier questions play to GEP's integration pedigree if left unanswered.
Implementation partner is in flux (EY vs Accenture/KPMG), and SAMI ties partner choice to the SRM decision, adding delivery/pricing uncertainty.
CloseDate already slipped once (29 Jun to 29 Aug); an end-July selection leaves ~30 days for hosting/InfoSec/legal, so 29 Aug is optimistic.
AEC on-prem hosting plus InfoSec/DC review are open and unscheduled, and defense data-residency raises the compliance bar.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and two-way: buyer ran a full demo 23 Jun, is chasing revised proposals, and set an end-July decision. Momentum is real but arms-length and single-threaded through procurement.
items: Buyer actively requesting revised AEC-hosting and partner-based proposals (Franck emails 16-22 Jun)
End-of-July decision window stated by buyer (Next_Step 03 Jul)
Single-threaded through procurement; no champion and no budget-owner contact

items.action: Submit one clean revised proposal covering AEC on-prem hosting and Accenture/KPMG partner quotes.
items.trigger: Franck asked twice when to expect the AEC-hosting and Accenture/KPMG proposals
items.expected_effect: Removes the buyer's biggest open blocker and denies GEP a delivery/hosting tiebreaker before selection.

items.action: Map and reach the SAMI budget owner above Franck before the end-July decision.
items.trigger: Executive probed approvals on the demo but no budget owner is on record
items.expected_effect: Exposes commercial decision factors and lets a Zycus VP align with power before the award.

items.action: Deliver written answers to the open S/4HANA and defense-supplier certification queries.
items.trigger: Buyer flagged tech queries needing response and probed classified-material handling
items.expected_effect: Closes the integration gap GEP would otherwise exploit and protects the fit score.

items.action: Run a focused session tying Merlin agentic AI to SAMI localization and defense-supplier pain.
items.trigger: Buyer noted core flows are not AI-dependent; AIS is rep-driven not buyer-stated
items.expected_effect: Converts the AI wedge from narrative to decision criterion and lifts differentiation.

items.action: Arrange ST Engineering and A&D OEM reference calls to de-risk defense credibility.
items.trigger: SAMI requested the A&D OEM reference list and STE was pivotal in shortlisting
items.expected_effect: Third-party proof of defense delivery hardens the case for the selection committee.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of three finalists in SAMI's $2.2M Saudi defense S2P with a strong 23 Jun demo and an end-July decision; the only things between us and the win are an unmapped budget owner, an unproven defense-integration/hosting story, and no internal champion in an arms-length RFP.

win_position.text: Earns roughly 58: shortlisted to three with a demo the rep read as 'good' and a full-suite marquee defense account, but held below 65 by an arms-length RFP with no champion and an unmapped budget owner.
SAMI is actively pulling revised AEC-hosting and Accenture/KPMG partner proposals (Franck emails 16-22 Jun), a Zycus-specific buying motion that signals genuine intent.
GEP Worldwide's defense/ERP-integration pedigree directly answers SAMI's open S/4HANA and classified-supplier questions, so leaving them unanswered risks handing GEP the edge (Avoma 23 Jun).
No budget owner above Franck is engaged and there is no internal champion, so a late executive can reset commercial terms or vendor preference weeks before the award (Avoma 23 Jun; CRM 15 May).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-driven cadence: SAMI ran the 23 Jun SRM demo, requested revised proposals, and set an end-of-July evaluation close (Next_Step 03 Jul).
CloseDate already slipped from 29 Jun to 29 Aug (field history 07 Jun) and two Zycus deliverables (AEC proposal, partner quotes) are now past their promised end-of-June week.

customer_commitment.text: SAMI committed to select the SRM and implementor together by end of July and is coordinating hosting via its own AEC subsidiary, showing real internal investment (Franck 19 Jun).
Commitment is procurement-led and arms-length; no C-level sponsor or champion has surfaced, so buy-in above the evaluation team is unproven.

deal_risk.text: Timeline risk: an end-July selection leaves only ~30 days to a 29 Aug close for partner selection, AEC on-prem hosting, InfoSec/DC, and legal.
Integration and partner risk: open defense-grade S/4HANA questions plus an unsettled EY-vs-Accenture/KPMG delivery model could tip a close race to GEP.

champion_engaged.evidence: No champion; Franck explicitly does not fit the role (CRM 15 May).

economic_buyer_engaged.evidence: Budget owner above Franck unmapped and unengaged (Avoma 23 Jun).

timeline_slip.evidence: CloseDate slipped 29 Jun to 29 Aug; deliverables past promised date.

scope_change.to: $2.2M full Source-to-Pay suite (16 modules incl. Merlin/Agentic AI)
scope_change.detail: Amount rose 1,200,000 to 2,200,000 on 2026-03-25 as scope expanded to the full S2P suite; an expanding, not defensive, scope.

expansion_context.note: New-business defense RFP; no prior Closed-Won on SAMI, so executive access is not inherited and mapping the budget owner is a genuine gap.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer is running Zycus-specific next steps (AEC hosting, partner quotes, demo follow-ups) and rep reads the demo position as 'good', but the RFP is arms-length with no explicit Zycus preference stated (Franck emails 16-22 Jun; Next_Step 03 Jul).

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (defense supply-chain complexity, Vision 2030 localization/GAMI compliance, classified-material handling) but no quantified savings, cost, or cycle-time figure stated (Avoma 23 Jun).

critical_signals.text: Zycus is one of three finalists in a formal Saudi defense RFP, with GEP the peer threat whose integration pedigree answers SAMI's open S/4HANA and defense-supplier questions.
The 23 Jun SRM live demo landed well, but left open defense-grade integration, classified-material handling, and hosting questions that still need written answers.
SAMI now wants Zycus hosted on-premise at its own AEC defense subsidiary data centre and priced through Accenture or KPMG rather than EY.
The budget owner who must sign the $2.2M sits above procurement advisor Franck and remains unmapped weeks before the end-July decision.
Revised partner-based quotes and an AEC-hosting proposal are the buyer's stated gate to concluding evaluations, and both are past Zycus's promised end-of-June delivery.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 63 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 46 is anchored on the stage win (63), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Zycus is one of three finalists in SAMI's $2.2M Saudi defense S2P with a strong 23 Jun demo and an end-July decision; the only things between us and the win are an unmapped budget owner, an unproven defense-integration/hosting story, and no internal champion in an arms-length RFP.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.

bullets.text: Earns roughly 58: shortlisted to three with a demo the rep read as 'good' and a full-suite marquee defense account, but held below 65 by an arms-length RFP with no champion and an unmapped budget owner.
SAMI is actively pulling revised AEC-hosting and Accenture/KPMG partner proposals (Franck emails 16-22 Jun), a Zycus-specific buying motion that signals genuine intent.
GEP Worldwide's defense/ERP-integration pedigree directly answers SAMI's open S/4HANA and classified-supplier questions, so leaving them unanswered risks handing GEP the edge (Avoma 23 Jun).
No budget owner above Franck is engaged and there is no internal champion, so a late executive can reset commercial terms or vendor preference weeks before the award (Avoma 23 Jun; CRM 15 May).

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 63.

bullets.text: Buyer-driven cadence: SAMI ran the 23 Jun SRM demo, requested revised proposals, and set an end-of-July evaluation close (Next_Step 03 Jul).
CloseDate already slipped from 29 Jun to 29 Aug (field history 07 Jun) and two Zycus deliverables (AEC proposal, partner quotes) are now past their promised end-of-June week.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Timeline risk: an end-July selection leaves only ~30 days to a 29 Aug close for partner selection, AEC on-prem hosting, InfoSec/DC, and legal.
Integration and partner risk: open defense-grade S/4HANA questions plus an unsettled EY-vs-Accenture/KPMG delivery model could tip a close race to GEP.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Live 3-finalist formal defense RFP.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: SAMI committed to select the SRM and implementor together by end of July and is coordinating hosting via its own AEC subsidiary, showing real internal investment (Franck 19 Jun).
Commitment is procurement-led and arms-length; no C-level sponsor or champion has surfaced, so buy-in above the evaluation team is unproven.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Submit one clean revised proposal covering AEC on-prem hosting and Accenture/KPMG partner quotes. (by 10 Jul)
Map and reach the SAMI budget owner above Franck before the end-July decision. (by 14 Jul)
Deliver written answers to the open S/4HANA and defense-supplier certification queries. (by 17 Jul)

calls_omitted.reason: no transcript available in this sweep
no transcript available in this sweep

gaps: Economic buyer / budget owner above procurement is unmapped (no C-level SAMI contact in any role, call, or task).
No quantified ROI/value case stated by the buyer.
Two early calls (24 Feb, 13 Jan) had no transcript this sweep, limiting historical competitor-landscape detail.
